nodes.name: Slack Trigger
nodes.notes: Listens for new messages in #it-support. Ignores bot messages to prevent loops.

nodes.name: Extract Message
nodes.notes: Extracts message text, user info, and generates a ticket ID

nodes.name: Create Ticket in Supabase
nodes.notes: Inserts new ticket record into Supabase tickets table

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT Support Triage Agent for a mid-size technology company (15,000 employees).

Organization Context:
- Azure AD for identity, Okta for MFA, Cisco AnyConnect for VPN, SCCM/Intune for endpoint management, Slack for communication
- SLAs: P1=1h response/4h resolve, P2=4h/24h, P3=8h/3d, P4=24h/5d

Given a user's message, respond with ONLY a valid JSON object (no markdown, no backticks, no extra text):

{"category": "access_identity|connectivity|software|hardware|email_collaboration|security|service_request", "subcategory": "<specific issue>", "intent": "<machine_readable_intent>", "priority": "P1|P2|P3|P4", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "summary": "<1-2 sentences>", "search_terms": "<3-5 keywords>", "is_security_incident": true|false, "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}

PRIORITY: P1=service down/security/executive/10+ users. P2=user blocked. P3=inconvenienced but working. P4=enhancement/question.
SECURITY: phishing/malware/suspicious email/unauthorized access -> is_security_incident: true, ALWAYS.
CONFIDENCE: >0.90 clear intent, 0.80-0.90 likely, 0.60-0.80 uncertain, <0.60 too vague.

nodes.name: Triage Agent
nodes.notes: Classifies the ticket using Groq/Llama 3.3 70B. Outputs structured JSON with intent, priority, confidence.

nodes.name: Groq Chat Model (Triage)
nodes.notes: Groq Llama 3.3 70B - low temperature for consistent classification

nodes.name: Parse Classification
nodes.notes: Parses JSON from Groq response. Handles malformed output, sets defaults, enforces security keyword checks.

nodes.name: Search Knowledge Base
nodes.notes: Calls search_kb_text RPC function in Supabase. Returns top 3 matching KB articles by full-text relevance.

nodes.name: Fetch Policy Rules
nodes.notes: Fetches the policy rule for this intent from Supabase

nodes.name: Check Policy & Route
nodes.notes: Routing logic: security->escalate, low confidence->human, L3+threshold->lights_out, L2->hitl, default->human_triage

nodes.name: Route Switch
nodes.notes: Routes to: 0=Lights-Out, 1=HITL, 2=Escalate, 3=Human Triage

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT Resolution Agent. Given a ticket and KB articles, generate a resolution plan and invoke tools.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{"resolution_plan": [{"step": 1, "action": "desc", "tool": "tool_name or null", "parameters": {}}], "tools_to_invoke": [{"tool": "name", "parameters": {}, "risk_level": "low"}], "user_message": "friendly message to user", "resolution_summary": "internal summary", "kb_citations": ["KB-XXXX: Title"], "confidence_in_resolution": 0.95, "requires_followup": false, "followup_note": ""}

Rules: Only use tools in tools_allowed. Always cite KB articles. User messages should be warm and clear. Never invent steps not in KB.

nodes.name: Resolver Agent (Lights-Out)
nodes.notes: Generates resolution plan for autonomous execution

nodes.name: Groq Chat Model (Resolver)
nodes.notes: Groq Llama 3.3 70B for resolution generation

nodes.name: Parse Resolution (LO)
nodes.notes: Parses resolver output for lights-out path

nodes.name: Execute Mock Tool
nodes.notes: Calls mock API server to simulate enterprise tool execution

parameters.text: ={{ $('Parse Resolution (LO)').item.json.resolution.user_message || 'Your issue has been resolved. If you need further help, reply here.' }}

nodes.name: Slack Reply (Resolved)
nodes.notes: Sends resolution message to user in Slack thread

parameters.jsonBody: ={"status": "resolved", "resolution_mode": "lights_out", "resolution_summary": "{{ $('Parse Resolution (LO)').item.json.resolution.resolution_summary || 'Auto-resolved' }}", "resolved_by": "ai_agent", "resolved_at": "{{ new Date().toISOString() }}", "intent": "{{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.intent }}", "priority": "{{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.priority }}", "confidence": {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.confidence }}}

nodes.name: Update Ticket (Resolved)
nodes.notes: Updates ticket status to resolved in Supabase

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT Resolution Agent. Generate a proposed resolution that a human will review before execution.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{"proposed_action": "clear description of what will be done", "tools_to_invoke": [{"tool": "name", "parameters": {}, "risk_level": "low|medium"}], "rationale": "why this is the right fix", "kb_citations": ["KB-XXXX: Title"], "user_message_draft": "draft message to send user after approval", "confidence_in_resolution": 0.85}

Rules: Only use tools in tools_allowed. Cite KB articles. Be specific about what will happen.

nodes.name: Resolver Agent (HITL)
nodes.notes: Generates proposed resolution for human approval

nodes.name: Groq Chat Model (HITL Resolver)
nodes.notes: Groq Llama 3.3 70B for HITL resolution proposals

nodes.name: Parse Resolution (HITL)
nodes.notes: Parses HITL proposal output

nodes.name: Post to #agent-approvals
nodes.notes: Posts approval request to #agent-approvals channel with full context

nodes.name: Update Ticket (Awaiting)
nodes.notes: Sets ticket status to awaiting_approval

parameters.text:
=:hourglass: I've analyzed your issue and proposed a solution. It's been sent to our team for review. You'll hear back shortly.

Ticket ID: {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.ticket_id }}

nodes.name: Slack Ack (HITL)
nodes.notes: Acknowledges the user that their ticket is being reviewed

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT Escalation Agent. Package tickets for human handoff with maximum context.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON:
{"escalation_summary": "2-3 sentences", "what_was_tried": ["list"], "what_was_found": ["list"], "recommended_team": "channel name", "recommended_priority": "P1-P4", "context_for_agent": "full context paragraph", "suggested_next_steps": ["actionable steps"], "user_communication": "message for user"}

Rules: Include everything. For security incidents, no speculation - just facts. Set realistic timing expectations.

nodes.name: Escalation Agent
nodes.notes: Generates escalation context package

nodes.name: Groq Chat Model (Escalation)
nodes.notes: Groq Llama 3.3 70B for escalation packaging

nodes.name: Parse Escalation
nodes.notes: Parses escalation output. Forces security incidents to #secops.

nodes.name: Post Escalation
nodes.notes: Posts escalation context to #tier2-support or #secops

parameters.text:
={{ $json.escalation.user_communication || 'Your ticket has been escalated to our specialist team. We\'ll follow up shortly.' }}

Ticket ID: {{ $json.ticket_id }}

nodes.name: Slack Reply (Escalated)
nodes.notes: Tells user their ticket has been escalated

nodes.name: Update Ticket (Escalated)
nodes.notes: Updates ticket status to escalated

nodes.name: Post to #human-triage
nodes.notes: Posts low-confidence tickets to human triage channel

parameters.text:
=We've received your request. A team member will review it shortly.

Ticket ID: {{ $json.ticket_id }}

nodes.name: Slack Reply (Human Triage)
nodes.notes: Acknowledges user that a human will review

parameters.jsonBody: ={"ticket_id": "{{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.ticket_id }}", "agent_name": "triage_agent", "action": "classify_and_route", "input_summary": "{{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.raw_text.substring(0, 200) }}", "output_summary": "Route: {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.route }} | Intent: {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.intent }} | Priority: {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.priority }}", "confidence": {{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.classification.confidence }}, "policy_decision": "{{ $('Check Policy & Route').item.json.route }}"}

nodes.name: Audit Log (Triage)
nodes.notes: Logs triage classification and routing decision to audit trail
